stemHtml:
Refer to figure or CAP 698 figure 3.7
Why is headwind/tailwind not accounted for in the figure above?

options.a: Because the data is measured in reference to the ground.
options.b: Because the data is measured in reference to the air.
options.c: Wind is only ever accounted for when calculating obstacle clearance.
options.d: A certain amount of wind has already been accounted for in this graph.
